items.title: South Korea to spend $1T on more memory chip production and humanoid robots
items.summary: South Korea targets physical AI lead and commercial humanoid robots by 2028.

items.title: Gemini’s personalized AI image generation is now free for US users
items.summary: Google is expanding Gemini’s personalized AI image generation to eligible free users in the U.S., allowing the chatbot to create images based on your interests and data from connected Google apps.

items.title: Tidal won’t pay royalties on AI-generated music but isn’t banning it outright
items.summary: Tidal shared its new policies regarding AI-generated music today and how the platform plans to "protect artists" and "inform listeners." items.title: OpenAI is teasing new hardware… for Codex
items.summary: OpenAI is releasing some sort of device related to its AI-powered coding tool, Codex, on July 15th. items.title: Anthropic and Gov. Newsom forge deal allowing California government to use Claude at half price
items.summary: As Anthropic forges a closer relationship with the state of California, the federal government has made an enemy out of the OpenAI rival.

items.title: South Korean tech giants commit over $550B to ease ‘RAMageddon’
items.summary: The world's two largest memory chip companies vow to build more memory lab fabs as South Korea positions itself as an AI tech powerhouse country.

items.title: Arena, the AI leaderboard everyone uses, is now a $100M business
items.summary: The startup, which runs a popular free AI leaderboard, launched its commercial service just last September.

items.title: Cursor now has a mobile app for guiding your coding agent on the go
items.summary: Cursor has launched a new mobile app for remote oversight over coding agents.

items.title: Claude Meets Blackwell Ultra: Anthropic’s Models Now Run on NVIDIA GB300 in Azure
items.summary: Anthropic’s Claude models in Microsoft Foundry — hosted on Microsoft Azure and running on NVIDIA GB300 Blackwell Ultra GPUs — are now generally available, giving Azure-native enterprises a powerful new way to build autonomous and domain-specific AI agents. items.title: TIDAL cracks down on AI music by cutting off monetization
items.summary: In addition, TIDAL will use automated tools to remove AI-generated music that attempts to impersonate an artist or a group, the company said.

items.title: Ask an AI expert: What exactly is the full stack?
items.summary: A Google expert explains what it means to take a full-stack approach to AI and why it’s been the foundation of our AI work for so long.

items.title: Lawmakers want to ban AI companies from selling your health data
items.summary: A new proposal would ban the sale of Americans' health and location information to data brokers - including information people reveal to an AI chatbot like ChatGPT or Claude. items.summary: The startup, Proception, is taking a unique approach to collecting training data to tackle one of the hardest problems in robotics: hands.

items.title: The Lab Mistake That Might Revolutionize Computing

As it usually does at these things, the issue of AI came up, and Atwood didn't mince words. According to Deadline's recap, Atwoo

items.title: Apple Vision Pro exec is reportedly leaving for OpenAI
items.summary: Paul Meade, the Apple vice president in charge of the Vision Pro headset, is reportedly leaving the company to join OpenAI’s hardware team.

items.title: The fittest founder in the room got cancer. Here’s how he used AI to fight back.
items.summary: When confronted with cancer, Conno Christou fed everything tied to his regime — blood results, scan data, wearable output, journal entries — into Claude.

items.title: Why is Apple asking me to pay more for Big Tech’s AI obsession?
items.summary: Tim Cook recently said price increases were "unavoidable" and described the company's pricing as "unsustainable." The 16-inch MacBook Pro saw its price go up by $300. The 11-inch iPad Air went from $599 to $749. Even the HomePod Mini got a $30 bump to $129. Cook squarely placed the blame at the feet

items.title: ConlangCrafter Turns AI to Imagining Languages
items.summary: There are over 7,000 natural languages today, but that doesn’t stop people from occasionally making up completely new ones. These constructed languages, or conlangs , include Dothraki , Klingon , and various Elvish languages . Now, an AI model called ConlangCrafter is also capable of generating new

items.title: Asian AI startups launch Mythos-like  models as Anthropic’s export ban drags on
items.summary: New models are launching in Asia that promise Mythos-like capabilities without fear of an export ban. U.S. AI labs may never recover this enormous market.

items.title: Trump administration releases Anthropic Mythos to be used by more than 100 US companies, agencies
items.summary: Over 100 companies and government agencies are reportedly authorized to use Mythos 5, including their non-American employees.

items.title: Anthropic’s Mythos 5 is back
items.summary: After a rollercoaster negotiation process with the Trump administration that dragged on for two weeks, Anthropic's Mythos 5 is finally back in action - at least, somewhat, for a select group of organizations, according to a letter from the government to Anthropic that was viewed by The Verge. items.title: South Korea plans to train entire military as "drone warriors"
items.summary: Half-million-strong military will train on drones as “universal combat tool.”
